CrewTypes.Name: Assassins
CrewTypes.Hook: You're professional murderers—death is your business.
CrewTypes.Description: Assassins use murder, intimidation, and targeted violence to maintain control of their operations and earn coin.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a successful murder, ransom, or assassination operation.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Predators
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you use a stealth or deception plan to commit murder, take +1d to the engagement roll.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Deadly
SpecialAbilities.Description: Each PC may add +1 action rating to Hunt, Prowl, or Skirmish (up to a max rating of 3).

SpecialAbilities.Name: Death Veil
SpecialAbilities.Description: Due to hard-won experience or occult ritual, you don't take extra heat when killing is involved on a score.

SpecialAbilities.Name: No Traces
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you keep an operation quiet or make it look like an accident, you get half the rep value of the target (round up) instead of zero. When you end downtime with zero heat, take +1 rep.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Patron
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you advance your Tier, it costs half the coin it normally would.

SpecialAbilities.Name: War Dogs
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you're at war (-3 faction status), your crew does not suffer -1 hold and PCs still get two downtime activities, instead of just one.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Forged in the Fire
SpecialAbilities.Description: Each PC has been toughened by cruel experience. You get +1d to resistance rolls.

Upgrades.Name: Cohort (Gang or Expert)
Upgrades.Description: A cohort is a gang or a single expert NPC who works for your crew.

Upgrades.Name: Training
Upgrades.Description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a given xp track during downtime.

Upgrades.Name: Secure Lair
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has locks, alarms, and traps to thwart intruders. A second upgrade improves the defenses to include arcane measures.

Upgrades.Name: Hidden Lair
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has a secret location and is disguised to hide it from view.

Upgrades.Name: Vault
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has a secure vault, increasing your storage capacity for coin to 8. A second upgrade increases your capacity to 16.

Upgrades.Name: Quarters
Upgrades.Description: Your lair includes living quarters for the crew.

Upgrades.Name: Workshop
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has a workshop appointed with tools for tinkering and alchemy.

Upgrades.Name: Quality: Weapons
Upgrades.Description: Each upgrade improves the quality rating of all the PCs' weapons.

Upgrades.Name: Mastery
Upgrades.Description: Your crew has access to master level training. You may advance your PCs' action ratings to 4.

Upgrades.Name: Hardened
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box.

Upgrades.Name: Composed
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 stress box.

StartingUpgrades.Name: Quality: Weapons
StartingUpgrades.Description: Each upgrade improves the quality rating of all the PCs' weapons.

StartingUpgrades.Name: Cohort (Gang or Expert)
StartingUpgrades.Description: A cohort is a gang or a single expert NPC who works for your crew.

CrewTypes.Name: Bravos
CrewTypes.Hook: You're violent thugs—muscle for hire in the underworld.
CrewTypes.Description: Bravos use battle, extortion, and shows of force to protect their territory and earn coin.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a successful operation involving battle, extortion, or a show of force.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Deadly
SpecialAbilities.Description: Each PC may add +1 action rating to Hunt, Prowl, or Skirmish (up to a max rating of 3).

SpecialAbilities.Name: Forged in the Fire
SpecialAbilities.Description: Each PC has been toughened by cruel experience. You get +1d to resistance rolls.

SpecialAbilities.Name: War Dogs
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you're at war (-3 faction status), your crew does not suffer -1 hold and PCs still get two downtime activities, instead of just one.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Fearsome
SpecialAbilities.Description: [TODO: Fill in from core book — Bravos special ability]

SpecialAbilities.Name: Patron
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you advance your Tier, it costs half the coin it normally would.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Fiends
SpecialAbilities.Description: Fear is as good as respect. You may count each wanted level as if it were turf.

SpecialAbilities.Name: All Hands
SpecialAbilities.Description: During downtime, one of your cohorts may perform a downtime activity for the crew to acquire an asset, reduce heat, or work on a long-term project.

Upgrades.Name: Cohort (Gang or Expert)
Upgrades.Description: A cohort is a gang or a single expert NPC who works for your crew.

Upgrades.Name: Training
Upgrades.Description: If you have a Training upgrade, you earn 2 xp (instead of 1) when you train a given xp track during downtime.

Upgrades.Name: Prowess Training
Upgrades.Description: If you have Prowess Training, when you train Prowess during downtime, you mark 2 xp on the Prowess track (instead of just 1).

Upgrades.Name: Secure Lair

Upgrades.Name: Hidden Lair

Upgrades.Name: Vault
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has a secure vault, increasing your storage capacity for coin to 8. A second upgrade increases your capacity to 16.

Upgrades.Name: Quarters
Upgrades.Description: Your lair includes living quarters for the crew.

Upgrades.Name: Workshop
Upgrades.Description: Your lair has a workshop appointed with tools for tinkering and alchemy.

Upgrades.Name: Quality: Weapons
Upgrades.Description: Each upgrade improves the quality rating of all the PCs' weapons.

Upgrades.Name: Mastery
Upgrades.Description: Your crew has access to master level training. You may advance your PCs' action ratings to 4.

Upgrades.Name: Hardened
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box.

Upgrades.Name: Composed
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 stress box.

StartingUpgrades.Name: Prowess Training
StartingUpgrades.Description: If you have Prowess Training, when you train Prowess during downtime, you mark 2 xp on the Prowess track (instead of just 1).

StartingUpgrades.Name: Cohort (Gang or Expert)
StartingUpgrades.Description: A cohort is a gang or a single expert NPC who works for your crew. Start with a gang of Thugs.

CrewTypes.Name: Cultists
CrewTypes.Hook: You're devoted to a strange power—occult secrets and forbidden rituals are your trade.
CrewTypes.Description: Cultists use occult rituals, dark blessings, and supernatural threats to advance their esoteric agenda.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a successful operation involving consecration, sacrifice, or occult power.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Anointed
SpecialAbilities.Description: You gain +1d to resistance rolls against supernatural threats. You get +1d to healing rolls when you have supernatural harm.

CrewTypes.Name: Hawkers
CrewTypes.Hook: You're dealers in vice—pleasure and addiction are your stock in trade.
CrewTypes.Description: Hawkers sell contraband, vices, and illicit goods to the citizens of Doskvol, using social connections and product quality to build their empire.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a successful operation involving product supply, sale, or acquisition of new clients or contraband sources.

SpecialAbilities.Name: High Society
SpecialAbilities.Description: It's all about who you know. Take -1 heat during downtime and +1d to gather information about the city's elite.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Pitch
SpecialAbilities.Description: [TODO: Fill in from core book — Hawkers special ability]

SpecialAbilities.Name: Renown
SpecialAbilities.Description: [TODO: Fill in from core book — Hawkers special ability]

SpecialAbilities.Name: Trust
SpecialAbilities.Description: [TODO: Fill in from core book — Hawkers special ability]

SpecialAbilities.Name: Patron
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you advance your Tier, it costs half the coin it normally would.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Pack Rats
SpecialAbilities.Description: Your lair is a jumble of stolen items. When you roll to acquire an asset, take +1d.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Just Passing Through
SpecialAbilities.Description: During downtime, take -1 heat. When your heat is 4 or less, you get +1d to deceive people when you pass yourselves off as ordinary citizens.

Upgrades.Name: Cohort (Gang or Expert)
Upgrades.Description: A cohort is a gang or a single expert NPC who works for your crew.

Upgrades.Description: Each upgrade improves the quality rating of all the PCs' subterfuge supplies.

Upgrades.Name: Mastery

Upgrades.Name: Hardened
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 trauma box.

Upgrades.Name: Composed
Upgrades.Description: Each PC gets +1 stress box.

Upgrades.Name: Vice Den
Upgrades.Description: During downtime, roll dice equal to your Tier. You earn coin equal to the highest result, minus your heat.

StartingUpgrades.Name: Vice Den
StartingUpgrades.Description: During downtime, roll dice equal to your Tier. You earn coin equal to the highest result, minus your heat.

CrewTypes.Name: Shadows
CrewTypes.Hook: You're stealthy thieves—burglary and espionage are your stock in trade.
CrewTypes.Description: Shadows use stealth, infiltration, and burglary to steal secrets and treasure from the wealthy and powerful of Doskvol.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a successful burglary, robbery, or espionage operation.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Second Story
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you execute a clandestine infiltration, you get +1d to the engagement roll.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Synchronized
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you perform a group action, you may count multiple 6s from different rolls as a critical success.

SpecialAbilities.Name: No Traces

CrewTypes.Name: Smugglers
CrewTypes.Hook: You move contraband through the canals and back alleys—everyone needs something they can't get legally.
CrewTypes.Description: Smugglers move contraband, passengers, and illicit goods through Doskvol's waterways and hidden routes.
CrewTypes.ExperienceTrigger: Execute a smuggling operation or acquire new clients or contraband sources.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Boat House
SpecialAbilities.Description: You have a boat, a dock on a waterway, and a small shack to store boating supplies. A second upgrade improves the boat with armor and more cargo capacity.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Rigging
SpecialAbilities.Description: You get 2 free load in two of the equipment categories (weapons, implements, supplies, gear, documents, and tools).

SpecialAbilities.Name: Underground Maps and Passkeys
SpecialAbilities.Description: You have easy passage through the underground canals, tunnels, and basements of the city.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Camouflage
SpecialAbilities.Description: Your vehicles are perfectly concealed when at rest. They blend in as part of the environment, or as an uninteresting civilian vehicle.

SpecialAbilities.Name: Patron
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you advance your Tier, it costs half the coin it normally would.

SpecialAbilities.Name: No Traces
SpecialAbilities.Description: When you keep an operation quiet or make it look like an accident, you get half the rep value of the target (round up) instead of zero. When you end downtime with zero heat, take +1 rep.

SpecialAbilities.Name: All Hands
SpecialAbilities.Description: During downtime, one of your cohorts may perform a downtime activity for the crew to acquire an asset, reduce heat, or work on a long-term project.

Upgrades.Name: Boat House

Upgrades.Name: Carriage House
Upgrades.Description: You have a carriage, and draft animals to pull it, and a stable. A second upgrade improves the carriage with armor and larger, swifter steeds.
